title: dendrocnide subclausa
intro: Dendrocnide subclausa, also known as the giant stinging tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Urticaceae family and is native to the tropical forests of northern Australia. The plant contains urtic acid and other compounds that cause severe pain and inflammation when its sap comes into contact with human skin, but it also has medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever and rheumatism. The health benefits of Dendrocnide subclausa include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Dendrocnide subclausa are typically made from the plant's leaves or sap, and can be used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, although caution must be exercised due to the plant's potent and irritating compounds.
